quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: The Strait of Juan de Fuca, which you can see from Port Angeles, is named after a Greek sailor who sailed for Spain in 1592. His real name was Ioannis Phokas, but the Spanish version stuck! The strait is one of the most important waterways in the Pacific Northwest, connecting the Pacific Ocean to Puget Sound.

short_read.title: Why Hummingbirds Are Nature's Perfect Gardeners
short_read.teaser: These tiny jeweled birds visit up to 2,000 flowers every day, helping gardens bloom beautifully. Learn about the special relationship between hummingbirds and the flowers they love most.

medium_read.title: The Secret Lives of Roosevelt Elk in Olympic National Park
medium_read.teaser: The majestic elk that roam the Olympic Peninsula have fascinating family structures and behaviors. Discover how these gentle giants have thrived in the rainforests just beyond Port Angeles for thousands of years.

long_read.title: The Hidden Gardens of Budapest: A Journey Through Time
long_read.teaser: From the stunning Margaret Island to secret courtyards, Budapest's gardens tell stories of resilience and beauty through centuries of history. This gentle tour takes you through the green heart of Hungary's capital city.

port_angeles.title: Harbor Seal Pupping Season is Here
port_angeles.description: June through September is pupping season for harbor seals along the Strait of Juan de Fuca. You might spot adorable seal pups resting on rocks near Ediz Hook or along the shoreline. Remember to watch from a distance—mothers often leave pups alone while they hunt for food, and they'll come back!

puzzle_1.question: A hummingbird's heart beats about ___ times per minute while flying.
puzzle_1.correct_response: Wonderful! You're exactly right—1,200 beats per minute! That's about 20 times faster than a human heart.
puzzle_1.close_response: You're very close! A hummingbird's heart beats about 1,200 times per minute—incredibly fast for such a tiny bird!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: These tiny birds have amazingly fast hearts! A hummingbird's heart beats about 1,200 times per minute while flying—that's 20 beats every second!
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: Hummingbirds have the fastest metabolism of any animal on Earth. To fuel their rapid heartbeat and flight, they must eat about half their body weight in sugar each day by visiting hundreds of flowers.

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'butterfly'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Gyönyörű! That's right—'pillangó' is butterfly! Such a lovely, musical word.
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The Hungarian word for butterfly is 'pillangó'—doesn't it sound beautiful? (Madár means bird, virág means flower, and méhecske means little bee.)
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: Hungarian is a unique language in Europe, related to Finnish and Estonian rather than neighboring languages. Many Hungarian words for nature are especially poetic and musical.

puzzle_3.question: What special ability do orcas (killer whales) have that helps them hunt in the Strait of Juan de Fuca?
options: They can see in complete darkness
They use echolocation like sonar to find prey
They can hold their breath for two hours
They can swim faster than any other animal
puzzle_3.correct_response: Perfect! Orcas use echolocation, sending out clicking sounds that bounce back to create a 'sound picture' of their surroundings. It's like having built-in sonar that lets them hunt salmon and navigate even in murky water!
puzzle_3.wrong_response: Orcas are amazing hunters because they use echolocation! They send out clicking sounds that bounce back, creating a 'sound picture' of everything around them—including their favorite food, salmon. It works like natural sonar!
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: The Southern Resident orcas that swim through the Strait of Juan de Fuca are an endangered population that relies heavily on Chinook salmon. Each orca has a unique call, like a family dialect, that helps them communicate with their pod.

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
